In this lesson, we're going to talk about why you need to test. When I say test, what I'm talking about is putting the ideas that you have for improving your funnel performance to the test. It's testing, testing the ideas, testing a new headline, testing a new offer. Testing these different elements is what allows you to scientifically and methodically improve the results that you're getting from your marketing funnel. The great thing with direct response marketing is that it's all numbers. It's data-driven. Direct response marketing is all about communication and math and numbers. The scientific portion of direct response is all about the data. We allow data to drive our decisions about what changes we make, what improvements we make, what tweaks we make to the website. The thing that I want you to think about right now is that the performance of your marketing funnel today, no matter what it is that you have right now, your existing funnel or when you have a funnel built on the six-figure funnel formula, today it's the worst that it's ever going to be. Because we're going to methodically and scientifically improve your results by testing new elements against what it is that you currently have. We're going to see if our new idea, what we test against what you have right now improves performance or hurts performance. If it hurts performance, then we're going to choose something else. If our idea improves performance based on the data, then we have an improved performance and now that new idea that we implemented becomes what's called the control, what we now test everything else against. You see, without actually testing, without testing like I'm going to show you in the rest of this module, you have no way of knowing what improves results and what suppresses results. You have no way of knowing if you're not testing if a new headline, a new offer, a new call to action. If it improved your results, if it hurt your results, is it improving sales? Is it hurting sales? What's it doing? See, it's the testing that gives you insight into what's working and what's not working. It's all about the data. You see, every tweak, every change that you make to your funnel, to your marketing, to your website must be driven by testing data. There is absolutely no place for assumptions or guesses in direct response marketing. You never want to assume that adding a video to a page, for example, will increase response or that adding audio to a page will increase response or that changing the headline will increase response. There's no room for assumptions. If all you're doing is making changes and assuming that things are going better, well, you have no way of knowing if they're going worse and you have no methodical and scientific way to improve results. That's why you need to test. As well, you need to test because it's the ongoing testing process that is responsible for the constant improvement of the performance of your funnel.
